content: This document provides structured planning guidelines for Level 3 (Intermediate Feature) tasks, focusing on comprehensive planning with creative phases and clear implementation strategies.
Continuously assess Level 3 tasks to determine if they should be downgraded to Level 1/2 (if simpler than expected) or upgraded to Level 4 (if there is system-wide impact or high complexity).
Identify whether creative phases for UI/UX Design, Architecture, or Algorithm Design are required based on triggers such as new UI components, system structure changes, or performance-critical logic.
Verify planning is complete by ensuring requirements are finalized, components are identified, design decisions are made, and implementation, testing, and documentation plans are ready.
The implementation process should follow structured phases: Setup, Core Implementation, UI Implementation, Testing, and Documentation.
For Level 3 tasks, ensure integration with the Memory Bank by creating a comprehensive projectbrief.md, detailed activeContext.md, structured tasks.md, and detailed progress.md.
Level 3 planning requires comprehensive design but should avoid over-engineering, focusing on delivering maintainable and well-documented features.
